skill.name: javascript-testing-patterns
skill.description: Guides JavaScript and TypeScript teams through practical testing patterns for Jest, Vitest, Testing Library, Supertest, mocks, fixtures, coverage, and TDD workflows.
skill.summary: Create maintainable JavaScript and TypeScript tests with modern framework patterns.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high indicators, but review found a single Markdown guide with TypeScript documentation examples, not an executable skill payload. Network, email, environment variable, and database cleanup examples are legitimate testing topics, but they create medium risk if copied into a real project without test isolation and secret hygiene.

medium_findings.title: Network and Email Examples Require Isolation
medium_findings.description: The guide includes sample fetch calls to an external API and a nodemailer transport that reads SMTP values from environment variables. This is appropriate test documentation, but copied examples could contact external services or expose email credentials if used outside mocked test environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network and environment access patterns are directly present in documentation examples. Confidence is below very high because the file is Markdown guidance and does not execute these calls itself.

medium_findings.title: Database Cleanup Examples Can Be Destructive
medium_findings.description: The integration test section shows test database setup and cleanup, including dropping and truncating a users table. The intent is legitimate, but these statements are dangerous if pointed at a shared or production database.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SQL cleanup operations are visible and potentially destructive in a copied test setup. The surrounding context names a test database, so the risk depends on user configuration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Hits Are Markdown Code Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick detections correspond to Markdown fenced TypeScript examples and package script snippets. No evidence found that the skill itself runs shell commands or evaluates user-controlled commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are code fences and configuration examples in a Markdown guide. There is no executable wrapper, script file, or command invocation path in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Detections Are Relative Import Examples
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts are relative imports used in sample tests, such as importing app, database, logger, or fixtures from neighboring source directories. No evidence found of arbitrary file reads or writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sequences appear inside normal TypeScript import paths. They do not take user input or access filesystem APIs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged words such as hash password, hashed_password, token, and password fields in testing examples. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, insecure random generation, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found password and token terminology in sample assertions, not weak cryptographic APIs. The finding is likely generated by broad keyword matching.

dangerous_patterns.title: External HTTP Calls in Sample Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The async service example calls a hardcoded API URL with fetch. Users should mock this pattern in tests and avoid sending real user data to example endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded fetch calls are clear, but they are part of a documentation example rather than active skill code.

dangerous_patterns.title: SMTP Credentials Read From Environment Variables
dangerous_patterns.description: The email service example reads SMTP host, user, password, and from address from environment variables. This is common practice, but tests should mock email delivery and protect secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable access is explicit and tied to email sending. The surrounding mocking section reduces malicious-intent confidence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive SQL Cleanup in Integration Test Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The repository test example includes table drop and truncate cleanup. These commands should only run against isolated test databases.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The SQL statements are visible and destructive, but the sample connection is named test_db and appears intended for testing isolation.

content.user_title: Build JavaScript Tests With Modern Patterns
content.value_statement: JavaScript teams often need consistent test patterns across unit, integration, frontend, and end-to-end work. This skill provides practical examples for Jest, Vitest, Testing Library, mocks, fixtures, coverage, and TDD.
actual_capabilities: Shows Jest and Vitest configuration patterns for TypeScript projects.
Explains unit tests for pure functions, classes, and async services.
Demonstrates mocking modules, spies, HTTP calls, and email services.
Covers API, database, React component, hook, fixture, and snapshot testing.
Includes coverage script examples and common test organization patterns.
limitations: It is a Markdown guide and does not install or run testing tools automatically.
Examples may need adaptation for each project structure and framework version.
Some integration examples require isolated test databases and mocked services.
It does not replace security review for tests that handle secrets or real user data.

use_cases.title: Add Unit Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Use the patterns to create focused tests for functions, classes, async behavior, and error handling.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Testing
use_cases.description: Use the guide to align project conventions for fixtures, mocks, coverage thresholds, and test organization.

use_cases.title: Improve Integration Confidence
use_cases.description: Use the API and database examples to design test suites that verify service behavior across boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Unit Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to write unit tests for this JavaScript or TypeScript function. Include normal cases, edge cases, and error cases.

prompt_templates.title: Mock an External Dependency
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to test this module while mocking network, email, database, or logging dependencies. Keep the tests deterministic.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Integration Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to plan integration tests for this API. Include setup, teardown, authentication, validation, and data isolation.

prompt_templates.title: Build a TDD Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to propose a TDD workflow for this feature. Include test names, fixtures, mocks, coverage targets, and refactoring checkpoints.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for tests for a calculator utility.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest grouped unit tests for addition, division, zero handling, negative numbers, decimal results, and thrown errors.

output_examples.input: A team needs API integration tests for user routes.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline setup and teardown, create-user checks, validation failures, duplicate email behavior, authentication checks, and response assertions.

output_examples.input: A React component accepts form input and submits user data.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend Testing Library tests for rendering fields, changing values, submitting the form, and asserting the submit handler payload.

best_practices: Mock external services unless a test explicitly requires an isolated integration environment.
Keep fixtures realistic but free of production secrets or real customer data.
Use descriptive test names and cover behavior, edge cases, and error paths.
anti_patterns: Do not run database cleanup examples against shared or production databases.
Do not let tests send real email or call real payment, analytics, or user APIs by default.
Do not assert implementation details when user-visible behavior is the real contract.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples that an AI assistant can adapt to your project.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Jest, Vitest, Testing Library, Supertest, faker, and common JavaScript testing workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use TypeScript-style configuration, imports, types, and test structure.

faq.question: Is it safe for production systems?
faq.answer: Use caution. Integration examples must be adapted to isolated test systems before execution.

faq.question: Does it include frontend testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes React component and hook testing patterns with Testing Library.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
